channel.title: BFG Blog
channel.description: Guides, tips, and insights across privacy-first iOS apps for finance, education, health, fitness, travel, and weather.

item.title: Year 4 Multiplication Tables Check (MTC): Everything Parents Need to Know
item.description: A complete guide to the Year 4 Multiplication Tables Check (MTC) in England. What it is, when it happens, how it's scored, and how to help your child prepare.

item.title: Why Does a Weather App Cost More Than It Used To?
item.description: Weather data comes from government agencies, so why do weather apps charge $5-10 per month? Here's where the money actually goes and what the alternatives are.

item.title: Why "Things to Do Near Me" Search Results Are So Bad (and What Would Be Better)
item.description: Google Maps shows paid placements, TripAdvisor shows tourist traps, and Yelp barely works outside the US. Here's why 'things to do near me' results are broken and what a better system would look like.

item.title: Why Children Forget Their Times Tables Over Summer (and How to Prevent It)
item.description: The summer slide in maths is real. Here's the cognitive science behind why children lose times tables fluency over long breaks, and practical ways to prevent it without worksheets.

item.title: What Children Find Online (and Why Parental Controls Alone Won't Fix It)
item.description: Parental controls filter what apps kids can open, but they can't filter what kids absorb through friends, school, and social contexts. A better content strategy works where restrictions alone fail.

item.title: What Is the Language Aptitude Test (LAT) in the 11+?
item.description: A clear explanation of the Language Aptitude Test (LAT) used in the 11+ exam for specialist language schools. What it tests, how it works, and how to prepare.

item.title: 11+ Verbal Reasoning: Tips, Question Types, and How to Practice
item.description: A practical guide to 11+ verbal reasoning question types, proven tips for improving scores, and the best ways to practise at home for grammar school entrance exams.

item.title: How to Build a Travel Map From Your Phone Photos
item.description: Your phone photos contain GPS data that can automatically map everywhere you've been. Here's how photo geotagging works and how to turn your library into a travel journal.

item.title: How to Build a Travel Journal From Photos You Already Have — No Location Sharing Required
item.description: Your photos already contain a complete travel record. Learn how EXIF GPS data works, why you don't need always-on location tracking, and how on-device photo analysis can reconstruct entire trips privately.

item.title: What Is Training Load and How It Prevents Running Injuries
item.description: Understanding training load is the single most effective way to avoid running injuries. Here's how the acute-to-chronic workload ratio works and how to use it.

item.title: Best Ways to Learn Times Tables (Backed by Research)
item.description: Evidence-based strategies for teaching times tables to primary school children. Adaptive practice, spaced repetition, and why gamification works.

item.title: Switching Period Trackers: What to Look for Before You Move Your Data
item.description: A practical guide for anyone switching period tracker apps. Covers feature checklists, data portability, privacy red flags, Apple Health backup, and what on-device really means.

item.title: How to Research Stocks Without Bloomberg
item.description: Free and affordable alternatives to Bloomberg Terminal for retail investors. Source-linked financial data, SEC filings, and global macro on your phone.

item.title: Sparks Studios vs Toca Boca: Creative Apps for Kids Compared
item.description: Comparing Sparks Studios and Toca Boca for children aged 4 to 12. Creation tools vs play worlds, pricing, privacy, and which approach builds real skills.

item.title: Screen Time Without the Guilt: A Practical Guide for Parents
item.description: Parents report screen time as their biggest source of guilt. Here is what the research actually says, why rigid rules backfire, and practical strategies that work.

item.title: How to Reset Your Child's Screen Time Habits Without Going Cold Turkey
item.description: Screen time has spiralled and cold turkey feels impossible. Here are gradual, research-backed strategies to reset your child's device habits without the meltdowns.

item.title: School Admissions in England: 10 Things Every Parent Should Know
item.description: Practical tips for navigating school admissions in England — from understanding catchment areas to waiting lists, appeals, and grammar school options.

item.title: The Parent's Guide to Choosing Safe Apps for Kids (No Ads, No Tracking)
item.description: How to evaluate whether a kids' app is truly safe. A practical checklist for privacy, ads, in-app purchases, and data collection — plus what to look for in the App Store.

item.title: The Best Running Apps That Don't Charge Monthly in 2026
item.description: Tired of paying monthly just to see your own GPS data? Here are the best running and fitness apps that use a one-time purchase or free model.

item.title: PhotoFlight vs Polarsteps: Which Travel Tracking App Should You Use?
item.description: Comparing PhotoFlight and Polarsteps for tracking your travels. Photo-based vs GPS tracking, pricing, privacy, and which approach suits different travellers.

item.title: Why Your Period Tracker's Privacy Policy Actually Matters
item.description: Period tracking apps handle some of the most intimate health data on your phone. Here's what to look for in a privacy policy and why on-device storage matters.

item.title: Why On-Device AI Makes Kids' Apps Fundamentally Safer
item.description: Cloud AI means your child's prompts, drawings, and stories go to a server. On-device AI keeps everything local. Here's why that matters for children's apps.

item.title: KS2 SATs Maths: How to Prepare Without the Stress
item.description: Practical advice for KS2 SATs maths preparation. What to expect from the three papers, how to practise effectively at home, and how to keep your child confident.

item.title: Congress Stock Trading: How to Track What Politicians Are Buying
item.description: How to track Congress stock trades, why insider selling ratios matter, and free tools to follow what US politicians are buying and selling in the stock market.

item.title: How to Verify Financial Data Before Making Investment Decisions
item.description: Retail investors often make decisions based on unreliable data. Here's a practical guide to verifying financial data sources before putting real money on the line.

item.title: How to Research International Stocks From Your Phone
item.description: You don't need a Bloomberg terminal to research stocks across 50+ countries. Here's how to access global financial data from your phone.

item.title: How to Read a 10-K Filing Without a Finance Degree
item.description: A beginner-friendly guide to reading SEC 10-K annual reports. Learn which sections matter, what red flags to watch for, and how to evaluate any company in 15 minutes.

item.title: How to Find Great Restaurants While Travelling (Without the Tourist Traps)
item.description: Practical strategies for finding authentic, high-quality restaurants while travelling abroad, from asking locals to using review velocity and context-aware apps.

item.title: How Community Weather Mesh Networks Give You Hyperlocal Forecasts
item.description: Traditional weather stations are miles apart. Community mesh networks use thousands of phones to fill the gaps. Here's how they work.

item.title: GL vs CEM: Understanding the Two 11+ Exam Formats
item.description: A clear comparison of GL Assessment and CEM 11+ exam formats. Which areas use which board, how the papers differ, and how to prepare for each.

item.title: What Happened When We Replaced YouTube With Creative Apps
item.description: One family's experience replacing YouTube with creative apps for their kids — and the surprising changes they noticed in behaviour, attention, and imagination.

item.title: Garmin Connect+ and the Fitness App Subscription Problem
item.description: Garmin announced Connect+, adding a subscription on top of expensive hardware. Here's why fitness platforms keep doing this — and what alternatives exist for runners who refuse to pay monthly.

item.title: Earn Screen Time: Why Rewarding Learning Works Better Than Hard Limits
item.description: Hard screen time limits create conflict and resentment. An earn-to-play model where children unlock fun apps by using learning apps builds autonomy and teaches time management.

item.title: Cyla vs Flo vs Clue: Period Tracker Privacy Comparison in 2026
item.description: A privacy-focused comparison of Cyla, Flo, and Clue period trackers in 2026. Covering data practices, features, pricing, and why period tracker privacy matters.

item.title: How Apple Watch Temperature Sensing Improves Cycle Tracking
item.description: Apple Watch Series 8+ includes a wrist temperature sensor that can help confirm ovulation. Here's how it works and which apps can use it.

item.title: Screen Time That's Actually Good: Creative Apps vs Passive Scrolling
item.description: Not all screen time is equal. Creative apps where kids draw, write stories, and compose music build skills. Here's how to tell the difference.

item.title: Why the Best Travel Recommendations Change With the Time of Day
item.description: A five-star restaurant is useless at 7am. Why context -- time of day, weather, proximity -- matters more than ratings when discovering things to do while travelling.

item.title: Cognithix vs Atom Learning: Which 11+ App Is Right for Your Child?
item.description: An honest comparison of Cognithix and Atom Learning for 11+ exam preparation. Features, pricing, LAT coverage, privacy, and which app suits different families.

item.title: Cloudmesh Weather vs CARROT Weather: Which Weather App Has Better Widgets?
item.description: A detailed comparison of Cloudmesh Weather and CARROT Weather, covering widget variety, pricing, privacy, data sources, and which app suits different users.

item.title: Best Weather Apps for the UK in 2026
item.description: A comparison of the best weather apps for UK users in 2026, covering accuracy, widgets, radar maps, and privacy for British weather conditions.

item.title: Best Travel Tracking Apps in 2026 — Map Your Trips Automatically
item.description: The best apps for tracking your travels and building a personal travel map in 2026, from manual journals to fully automatic GPS-based trackers.

item.title: Best 'Things to Do' Apps for Travellers in 2026
item.description: A fair comparison of the best apps for finding things to do while travelling in 2026, from Google Maps to Viator to context-aware newcomers.

item.title: Best Running Apps for Apple Watch in 2026
item.description: The best running apps for Apple Watch in 2026. Comparing Nike Run Club, Strava, Apple Fitness, Runkeeper, and PaceGrid on watch experience, metrics, and pricing.

item.title: Best Parental Control Apps for iPhone in 2026
item.description: A detailed comparison of parental control apps for iPhone in 2026. Apple Screen Time, Qustodio, Bark, Kidslox, OurPact, and Minua compared on features, privacy, and pricing.

item.title: The Best iPhone Weather Widgets in 2026
item.description: A guide to the best weather widgets for your iPhone home screen and lock screen, from minimal to feature-packed.

item.title: Best Finance Apps for DIY Investors in 2026
item.description: The best mobile apps for self-directed investors in 2026. Compare Yahoo Finance, Seeking Alpha, Bloomberg, Trading 212, and Kanesh on data quality, source transparency, and mobile experience.

item.title: The Complete Guide to 11+ Exam Preparation in 2026
item.description: Everything you need to know about preparing your child for the 11+ exam in 2026. Apps, books, strategies, and what to expect from grammar school entrance tests.

item.title: Arithmetix vs DoodleMaths: UK Maths Apps Compared for 2026
item.description: Comparing Arithmetix and DoodleMaths for UK primary maths practice. Pricing, curriculum coverage, privacy, and which app works better for different families.

item.title: Can Your Child Pass the 11+ Without an Expensive Tutor?
item.description: An honest look at whether 11+ tutoring is worth the cost. When self-study with apps, workbooks, and past papers is enough — and when a tutor genuinely helps.
